methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $200M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Fuse Energy is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by JC Btaiche, currently valued at $200M+ post-money with $78M raised to date. The nuclear energy sector comprises 11 comparable companies: 3 public (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

Stage-progression analysis is the applicable method here. Starting from the $200M last post-money valuation and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $400M. This method directly reflects observed step-up patterns in early-stage capital rounds within the sector.

Peer-median comparison is not applicable: while 7 same-sector peers exist, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier comparables yielded insufficient datapoints (fewer than 3), making a narrow peer-set median unreliable. A broad all-sector median would introduce unacceptable selection bias.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $340M to $460M, with a median of $400M. This range reflects a single-method foundation, which materially limits conviction. Key uncertainties include the absence of robust near-peer comparables at identical stage and valuation tier, and the reliance on step-up multiples that may not capture sector-specific capital dynamics. Additional data on recent private fundraising rounds or acquisition precedents in advanced nuclear would strengthen this assessment.
